page_title: Alesis Nitro Drum Module — Quick Practice Guide
title: Alesis Nitro Drum Module
subtitle: Quick Practice Guide
footer: Alesis Nitro Drum Module — Practice Reference

rows.desc: Cycles through the module's built-in drum kit sounds. Turn/press to browse, use PAGE/SELECT or the dial to confirm.
Sets the overall output level of the module (pads, click, and songs together). Turn to match headphone or amp level.
Mutes the drum pads while keeping the click and/or song audio playing — useful for listening back without your playing covering the track.

sections.heading: 2. Metronome Practice

rows.desc: Toggles the built-in metronome on/off. A steady click keeps your internal tempo honest.
Hold and turn the dial, or use the ▲▼ arrows, to set the click speed in BPM (beats per minute).

sections.tip: Start slow with a click on every beat, then gradually increase the tempo a few BPM at a time as the pattern feels solid.

rows.desc: Loads the module's built-in play-along tracks for practicing to real songs.
Navigate between songs and play/pause the currently loaded track.
Mutes an individual drum part (e.g., snare or hi-hat) from the song so you play that part live while the rest of the track continues.
Loads guided rhythm exercises that walk through beats and patterns at increasing difficulty.
Scrolls through the list of available exercises within Learning Mode.

sections.heading: 5. Pad Layout Reference
sections.intro: Use this map to find which pad triggers which sound when editing kits or following pattern exercises.

rows.desc: Crash cymbal pads, outer edges of the kit.
Rack and floor tom pads, arced across the middle.
Hi-hat pad and its open/closed pedal control, lower left.
Snare pad and rim-click zone, center.
Kick drum trigger, large and centered at the base of the kit.

sections.heading: 6. Recording Yourself

rows.desc: Arms and captures your playing (with click, song, or pattern if active) for later playback.
Stores the recording so it isn't lost when the module powers off or a new kit is loaded.
Begins and ends both recording and playback.

sections.tip: Play back every recording and check it against the click — this is the fastest way to spot rushing, dragging, or uneven fills.

routine.text: cycle KIT to a sound you like.
turn on CLICK and set a comfortable TEMPO.
run through basic rudiments on the pads with the click.
switch to SONG mode and use PLAY/PRACTICE to mute one part you want to isolate.
hit RECORD, play, then SAVE and listen back to check your timing.
